/**
 * CEF dictionary value structure wrapper.
 * This class models the CEF C API struct _cef_dictionary_value_t,
 * providing Java-accessible function pointers for its callback methods.
 * Note: This is a low-level binding; actual native memory management and
 * CEF runtime linkage must be handled externally (e.g., via JNI).
 */
public class CefDictionaryValueT {
    /**
     * Base structure.
     * cef_base_ref_counted_t base;
     */
    public CefBaseRefCountedT base = new CefBaseRefCountedT();

    /**
     * Returns true (1) if this object is valid. This object may become invalid
     * if the underlying data is owned by another object (e.g. list or
     * dictionary) and that other object is then modified or destroyed. Do not
     * call any other functions if this function returns false (0).
     */
    public long is_valid; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Returns true (1) if this object is currently owned by another object.
     */
    public long is_owned; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Returns true (1) if the values of this object are read-only. Some APIs may
     * expose read-only objects.
     */
    public long is_read_only; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Returns true (1) if this object and |that| object have the same underlying
     * data. If true (1) modifications to this object will also affect |that|
     * object and vice-versa.
     */
    public long is_same; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Returns true (1) if this object and |that| object have an equivalent
     * underlying value but are not necessarily the same object.
     */
    public long is_equal; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Returns a writable copy of this object. If |exclude_NULL_children| is true
     * (1) any NULL dictionaries or lists will be excluded from the copy.
     */
    public long copy; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, int) -> CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>>

    /**
     * Returns the number of values.
     */
    public long get_size; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>) -> size_t

    /**
     * Removes all values. Returns true (1) on success.
     */
    public long clear; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Returns true (1) if the current dictionary has a value for the given key.
     */
    public long has_key; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Reads all keys for this dictionary into the specified vector.
     * typedef struct _cef_string_list_t* cef_string_list_t;
     */
    public long get_keys; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_list_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Removes the value at the specified key. Returns true (1) if the value was
     * removed successfully.
     */
    public long remove; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Returns the value type for the specified key.
     */
    public long get_type; // CPointer<CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> cef_value_type_t>>

    /**
     * Returns the value at the specified key. For simple types the returned
     * value will copy existing data and modifications to the value will not
     * modify this object. For complex types (binary, dictionary and list) the
     * returned value will reference existing data and modifications to the value
     * will modify this object.
     */
    public long get_value; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> CPointer<_cef_value_t>>

    /**
     * Returns the value at the specified key as type bool.
     */
    public long get_bool; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Returns the value at the specified key as type int.
     */
    public long get_int; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Returns the value at the specified key as type double.
     */
    public long get_double; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> double>

    /**
     * Returns the value at the specified key as type string.
     *
     * The resulting string must be freed by calling cef_string_userfree_free().
     * typedef cef_string_userfree_utf16_t cef_string_userfree_t;
     * typedef cef_string_utf16_t* cef_string_userfree_utf16_t;
     */
    public long get_string; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> cef_string_userfree_t>

    /**
     * Returns the value at the specified key as type binary. The returned value
     * will reference existing data.
     */
    public long get_binary; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> CPointer<_cef_binary_value_t>>

    /**
     * Returns the value at the specified key as type dictionary. The returned
     * value will reference existing data and modifications to the value will
     * modify this object.
     */
    public long get_dictionary; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>>

    /**
     * Returns the value at the specified key as type list. The returned value
     * will reference existing data and modifications to the value will modify
     * this object.
     */
    public long get_list; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> CPointer<_cef_list_value_t>>

    /**
     * Sets the value at the specified key. Returns true (1) if the value was set
     * successfully. If |value| represents simple data then the underlying data
     * will be copied and modifications to |value| will not modify this object.
     * If |value| represents complex data (binary, dictionary or list) then the
     * underlying data will be referenced and modifications to |value| will
     * modify this object.
     */
    public long set_value; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>, CPointer<_cef_value_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Sets the value at the specified key as type null. Returns true (1) if the
     * value was set successfully.
     */
    public long set_null; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Sets the value at the specified key as type bool. Returns true (1) if the
     * value was set successfully.
     */
    public long set_bool; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>, int) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Sets the value at the specified key as type int. Returns true (1) if the
     * value was set successfully.
     */
    public long set_int; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>, int) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Sets the value at the specified key as type double. Returns true (1) if
     * the value was set successfully.
     */
    public long set_double; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>, double) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Sets the value at the specified key as type string. Returns true (1) if
     * the value was set successfully.
     */
    public long set_string; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Sets the value at the specified key as type binary. Returns true (1) if
     * the value was set successfully. If |value| is currently owned by another
     * object then the value will be copied and the |value| reference will not
     * change. Otherwise, ownership will be transferred to this object and the
     * |value| reference will be invalidated.
     */
    public long set_binary; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>, CPointer<_cef_binary_value_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Sets the value at the specified key as type dict. Returns true (1) if the
     * value was set successfully. If |value| is currently owned by another
     * object then the value will be copied and the |value| reference will not
     * change. Otherwise, ownership will be transferred to this object and the
     * |value| reference will be invalidated.
     */
    public long set_dictionary; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>, CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>) -> Int32>

    /**
     * Sets the value at the specified key as type list. Returns true (1) if the

    public long set_list; // CFunc<(CPointer<_cef_dictionary_value_t>, CPointer<cef_string_t>, CPointer<_cef_list_value_t>) -> Int32>
}

public class CodeMetrics {
    public let complexity: Int64
    public let maintainabilityIndex: Float64
    public let testCoverage: Float64
    public let duplicateLines: Int64
    public let commentRatio: Float64

    public init(complexity: Int64, maintainabilityIndex: Float64, testCoverage: Float64,
                duplicateLines: Int64, commentRatio: Float64) {
        this.complexity = complexity
        this.maintainabilityIndex = maintainabilityIndex
        this.testCoverage = testCoverage
        this.duplicateLines = duplicateLines
        this.commentRatio = commentRatio
    }

    public func toString(): toString {
        var result = ""
        result += "• 复杂度: ${complexity}\n"
        result += "• 可维护性指数: ${maintainabilityIndex}\n"
        result += "• 测试覆盖率: ${testCoverage}%\n"
        result += "• 重复行数: ${duplicateLines}\n"
        result += "• 注释比例: ${commentRatio}%"
        return result
    }
}

private func readEndOfCentralDirectoryRecord(
        zip4cjRaf: RandomAccessFile,
        rawIO: RawIO,
        zip4cjConfig: Zip4cjConfig
    ): EndOfCentralDirectoryRecord {
        var offsetEndOfCentralDirectory = locateOffsetOfEndOfCentralDirectory(zip4cjRaf)
        seekInCurrentPart(zip4cjRaf, offsetEndOfCentralDirectory + 4)

        var endOfCentralDirectoryRecord = EndOfCentralDirectoryRecord()
        endOfCentralDirectoryRecord.setSignature(HeaderSignature.END_OF_CENTRAL_DIRECTORY)
        endOfCentralDirectoryRecord.setNumberOfThisDisk(rawIO.readShortLittleEndian(zip4cjRaf))
        endOfCentralDirectoryRecord.setNumberOfThisDiskStartOfCentralDir(rawIO.readShortLittleEndian(zip4cjRaf))
        endOfCentralDirectoryRecord.setTotalNumberOfEntriesInCentralDirectoryOnThisDisk(
            rawIO.readShortLittleEndian(zip4cjRaf))
        endOfCentralDirectoryRecord.setTotalNumberOfEntriesInCentralDirectory(
            Int64(rawIO.readShortLittleEndian(zip4cjRaf)))
        endOfCentralDirectoryRecord.setSizeOfCentralDirectory(rawIO.readIntLittleEndian(zip4cjRaf))
        endOfCentralDirectoryRecord.setOffsetOfEndOfCentralDirectory(offsetEndOfCentralDirectory)

        zip4cjRaf.readFully(intBuff)
        endOfCentralDirectoryRecord.setOffsetOfStartOfCentralDirectory(rawIO.readLongLittleEndian(intBuff, 0))

        var commentLength = Int64(rawIO.readShortLittleEndian(zip4cjRaf))
        endOfCentralDirectoryRecord.setComment(readZipComment(zip4cjRaf, commentLength))

        zipModel.getOrThrow().setSplitArchive(endOfCentralDirectoryRecord.getNumberOfThisDisk() > 0)
        return endOfCentralDirectoryRecord
    }

public func convertToBytes(inputData: Any): Array<Byte> {
    return match (inputData) {
        case value: Bool => getBytes(value)
        case value: Rune => getBytes(value)
        case value: String => getBytes(value)

        case value: Int8 => getBytes(value)
        case value: Int16 => getBytes(value)
        case value: Int32 => getBytes(value)
        case value: Int64 => getBytes(value)

        case value: UInt8 => getBytes(value)
        case value: UInt16 => getBytes(value)
        case value: UInt32 => getBytes(value)
        case value: UInt64 => getBytes(value)

        // case value: Float16 => getBytes(value)
        case value: Float32 => getBytes(value)
        case value: Float64 => getBytes(value)

        case value: Array<Rune> => convertRunesToBytes(value)

        case value: Array<Int8> => convertInt8sToBytes(value)
        case value: Array<Int16> => convertInt16sToBytes(value)
        case value: Array<Int32> => convertInt32sToBytes(value)
        case value: Array<Int64> => convertInt64sToBytes(value)
        case value: Array<UInt8> => convertUInt8sToBytes(value)
        case value: Array<UInt16> => convertUInt16sToBytes(value)
        case value: Array<UInt32> => convertUInt32sToBytes(value)
        case value: Array<UInt64> => convertUInt64sToBytes(value)

        // case value: Array<Float16> => convertFloat16sToBytes(value)
        case value: Array<Float32> => convertFloat32sToBytes(value)
        case value: Array<Float64> => convertFloat64sToBytes(value)

        case _ => throw Exception("Unsupported type ${TypeInfo.of(inputData)}")
    }
}

           "       c.data_type,\n" +
           "       c.character_maximum_length,\n" +
           "       c.numeric_precision,\n" +
           "       c.numeric_scale,\n" +
           "       c.is_nullable,\n" +
           "       c.column_default,\n" +
           "       c.ordinal_position,\n" +
           "       c.extra\n" +
           "FROM information_schema.columns c\n" +
           "WHERE c.table_schema = '" + schemaName + "'\n" +
           "  AND c.table_name = '" + tableName + "'\n" +
           "ORDER BY c.ordinal_position;";
}

private func getTableSchemaQuery(schemaName: String, tableName: String) -> String {
        return """
            SELECT c.column_name,
                   c.data_type,
                   c.character_maximum_length,
                   c.numeric_precision,
                   c.numeric_scale,
                   c.is_nullable,
                   c.column_default,
                   c.ordinal_position,
                   c.extra
            FROM information_schema.columns c
            WHERE c.table_schema = '${schemaName}'
              AND c.table_name = '${tableName}'
            ORDER BY c.ordinal_position;
        """
    }

public enum SyncErrorType {
    case LatencyExceeded
    case DataConflict
    case ConnectionLost
    case ResourceExhausted
    case PerformanceDegradation
    case ConsistencyError
    case NetworkResilienceError
    case Custom(errorType: String)

    public func toString() -> String {
        switch self {
            case .LatencyExceeded: return "latency_exceeded"
            case .DataConflict: return "data_conflict"
            case .ConnectionLost: return "connection_lost"
            case .ResourceExhausted: return "resource_exhausted"
            case .PerformanceDegradation: return "performance_degradation"
            case .ConsistencyError: return "consistency_error"
            case .NetworkResilienceError: return "network_resilience_error"
            case .Custom(let errorType): return errorType
        }
    }
}

        this.colorClasses = ArrayList<ArrayList<V>>(n)
        this.positiveDegreeColorClasses = ArrayList<ArrayList<V>>(n)
        this.maxColorDegree = Array<Int64>(n, item: 0)
        this.minColorDegree = Array<Int64>(n, item: 0)
        this.colorDegree = HashMap<V, Int64>()
        this.coloring = HashMap<V, Int64>()

        for (_ in 0..n) {
            colorClasses.append(ArrayList<V>())
            positiveDegreeColorClasses.append(ArrayList<V>())
        }

        for (v in graph.vertexSet()) {
            colorClasses[alpha.getColors()[v]].append(v)
            colorDegree[v] = 0
            coloring[v] = alpha.getColors()[v]
        }

        lastUsedColor = alpha.getNumberColors() - 1
    }
}

private func unpackExtendedHeader(bytes: Array<Int8>, offset: Int32): Int32 {
        extendedHeaderLength = BufferTools.unpackSynchsafeInteger(
            bytes[Int64(offset)],
            bytes[Int64(offset) + 1],
            bytes[Int64(offset) + 2],
            bytes[Int64(offset) + 3]
        ) + 4
        extendedHeaderData = BufferTools.copyBuffer(
            bytes,
            Int64(offset) + 4,
            Int64(extendedHeaderLength)
        )

        return extendedHeaderLength
    }

public func gengammaPlot() {

    fplot({x => gengammaPDF(x, 5.0, -3.0)}, 0.2, 1.5).display_name("a=5,c=-3")
    hold(true)
    fplot({x => gengammaPDF(x, 3.0, -1.0)}, 0.2, 1.5).display_name("a=3,c=-1")
    fplot({x => gengammaPDF(x, 1.0, 3.0)},  0.2, 1.5).display_name("a=1,c=3")
    hold(false)
    title("Gengamma PDF Plot")
    xlabel("x")
    ylabel("f(x)")
    legend(true)
    save("./tests/imgs/stats/continuous/gengamma/gengammapdf.png", "png")
    clear()

    fplot({x => gengammaCDF(x, 5.0, -3.0)}, 0.2, 1.5).display_name("a=5,c=-3")
    hold(true)
    fplot({x => gengammaCDF(x, 3.0, -1.0)}, 0.2, 1.5).display_name("a=3,c=-1")
    fplot({x => gengammaCDF(x, 1.0, 3.0)},  0.2, 1.5).display_name("a=1,c=3")
    hold(false)
    title("Gengamma CDF Plot")
    xlabel("x")
    ylabel("F(x)")
    legend(true)
    save("./tests/imgs/stats/continuous/gengamma/gengammacdf.png", "png")
    clear()
}
